signals.reasoning: BTC is firm but not giving a clean enough edge to override the active veto.
ETH still looks constructive, but the move is already underway and not attractive enough for a fresh entry under veto.
SOL remains mixed, with price stable but open interest softening, which keeps it in watch-only territory.
TAO is one of the clearest crowded-upside fades on the board, with price up 5.3%, funding near +2.98 bps, and open interest up 22.91%, but veto keeps conviction capped.
FARTCOIN is up 10.08% with positive funding and rising open interest, so it still reads as one of the best crowded-upside fade candidates under this defensive regime.
WLD is strong but not crowded enough to justify a fade, and veto blocks chasing it on the long side.
ALT remains the best squeeze-long candidate because predicted funding is flipping negative while price is only modestly higher, but poor liquidity and veto keep this reduced-confidence only.
PENGU has upside momentum but no clean squeeze edge or rejection signal, so I would keep it watch-only.
PUMP is moving but not in a way that creates a clean, high-conviction continuation or fade entry under veto.
VVV is still extended enough to stay on the fade side, but it has not yet printed the clear peak rejection that would justify a stronger short call.
TON remains a strong future peak-rejection watch, but the chart has not actually cracked yet, so I would keep it at HOLD.
